context_note: Argentina vs Austria - WC2026 Group C, AT&T Stadium, Arlington TX. Argentina are massive favorites (Elo-based FTR: ARG 73.7% / Draw 17.7% / AUT 8.7%). RULE8 does NOT fire (draw 17.7% < 25% threshold). RULE7 fires: AUT at 8.7% (<15%) is extreme underdog — submit unshaded model estimates for all AUT-positive props and apply RULE15 (losing-team forwards SOT suppressed). Both teams' GD1 results: ARG beat Algeria 3-0 (confirmed high-line play, ARG caught 2+ offsides vs Algeria themselves); AUT beat Jordan 3-1 (BOMBSHELL: Sabitzer 0 SOT even as dominant player). Smarkets data pending for Q5 (RULE1: Argentina win = avg(crowd_estimate, Smarkets_market)).

poisson_goals.note: Neutral venue (no home_adv term). Source: data/processed/poisson_goals_coefs.json

ordered_logit_match_winner.note: Neutral venue — b_home term not applied. Source: data/processed/ordered_logit_coefs.json

arg_vs_alg_relevant_findings: ARG caught 2+ offsides vs Algeria themselves — confirms ARG plays AGGRESSIVE HIGH LINE
ARG scored 3 goals — strong attacking form
ARG scored in 1H (Q9: ARG scores 1H = YES) — confirms ARG attacks from the start
Algeria 2+ offsides (YES) further confirms ARG high-line trap works against opponents
No penalty/red card (Q6 NO) — ARG won cleanly without incidents
aut_vs_jor_relevant_findings: BOMBSHELL Q10: Sabitzer 0 SOT (crowd was 57%) even when Austria DOMINANT and WON 3-1
Q8: Austria <6 SOT (crowd 50%, outcome NO) — dominant team still fell below 6 SOT threshold
Q7: <4 total cards (crowd 44%, outcome NO) — dominant win had low card count
Q6: Jordan fewer corners than Austria (crowd 28%, outcome NO) — confirms dominant team gets more corners
Q3: Jordan fewer 2H SOT than Austria (crowd 27%, outcome NO) — confirms extreme underdog gets fewer 2H SOT
Q2: Jordan did NOT have more fouls than Austria (crowd 58%, outcome NO) — dominant team fouls similar or more
Q5: Austria scored in 2H (YES) — pattern holds: favorites score in 2H

rules_in_effect.RULE7: AUT 8.7% win prob (<15%) = EXTREME underdog. Submit unshaded model estimates for all AUT-positive props. Do not let crowd pull us toward midpoint for underdog props.
rules_in_effect.RULE15: Losing-team forward SOT suppressed. AUT <15% → 0.25-0.35 range regardless of model. FURTHER REVISED by Sabitzer GD1 data: 0 SOT even when dominant. Final: 0.15-0.22.
rules_in_effect.RULE8_NOT_FIRED: Draw only 17.7% — below 25% threshold. No general shrinkage of attacking props.
rules_in_effect.RULE1_PENDING: Q5 Argentina win: avg(crowd_estimate, Smarkets_market). Smarkets API pull needed (quota-controlled — must ask user permission).
rules_in_effect.RULE14: Consistency check: P(ARG more SOT) must be >= P(ARG more goals). Q8 (ARG 6+ SOT = 0.65) vs Q9 (ARG scores 2H ~ implies high SOT). Check passes.

Q1_aut_2plus_offsides.question: Will Austria be caught offside 2 or more times?
Q1_aut_2plus_offsides.method: Poisson model with ARG high-line adjustment. Base: lambda_aut_offsides = 1.3 (Poisson of typical underdog), P(>=2) = 0.373. Revised: lambda = 1.5 given ARG confirmed high-line play (ARG themselves caught 2+ offsides vs Algeria — direct evidence of trap defense). P(>=2) = 0.442. RULE7 applies: stay near model not crowd. Austria will chase late with runners = more offside risk.

Q2_aut_more_2h_sot_than_arg.question: Will Austria have more shots on target than Argentina in the second half?
Q2_aut_more_2h_sot_than_arg.method: Skellam: lambda_ARG_2H_SOT = 1.9254 * 0.55 * 3.7 = 3.92; lambda_AUT_2H_SOT = 0.6395 * 0.55 * 3.7 = 1.30. P(AUT > ARG in 2H SOT) = 7.65% from Skellam calculation. RULE7: extreme underdog -> AUT will have fewer opportunities in 2H. AUT-JOR CONFIRMS: Jordan (extreme underdog) had fewer 2H SOT than Austria (crowd 27%, outcome NO). Direct analogy. Crowd likely anchors at 0.20-0.28 due to compression; our estimate stays near model.
Q2_aut_more_2h_sot_than_arg.aut_jor_confirmation: Jordan (extreme underdog) had fewer 2H SOT than Austria — AUT vs JOR Q3 = NO at crowd 27%
Q2_aut_more_2h_sot_than_arg.edge_direction: STRONG NO (model 7.7% vs crowd ~24% = 16pp gap, RULE7)

Q3_2h_more_goals_than_1h.question: Will the second half have more goals than the first half?
Q3_2h_more_goals_than_1h.method: Poisson: lambda_1H = 2.565 * 0.45 = 1.154, lambda_2H = 2.565 * 0.55 = 1.411. P(2H > 1H) = 0.429. P(equal) = 0.263. Both teams have scored in 2H in GD1 (generic pattern). RULE8 not fired. No strong rule overrides. Model-based estimate.

Q4_aut_more_corners_than_arg.question: Will Austria finish with more corner kicks than Argentina?
Q4_aut_more_corners_than_arg.method: Poisson on corners: lambda_ARG_corners = 1.9254 * 2.8 = 5.39; lambda_AUT_corners = 0.6395 * 2.8 = 1.79. P(AUT > ARG) = 5.5%. AUT-JOR CONFIRMS: Jordan (extreme underdog) had FEWER corners than Austria (Q6 of AUT-JOR = NO at crowd 28%). Dominant team gets more corners. RULE7: stay near model.
Q4_aut_more_corners_than_arg.aut_jor_confirmation: Austria had more corners than Jordan (underdog) — Q6 of AUT-JOR = NO (crowd 28%, Jordan fewer corners)
Q4_aut_more_corners_than_arg.edge_direction: STRONG NO (model 5.5% vs crowd ~26% = 20pp gap, RULE7)

Q5_argentina_win.question: Will Argentina win the match?
Q5_argentina_win.method: RULE1: avg(crowd_consensus, Smarkets_market). Elo model: ARG 73.7%. Crowd formula pred: 0.514 + 0.61*(0.737-0.5) = 0.658. ALG match Q7: crowd priced ARG win at 75% (very close to our model 73.7%). Preliminary estimate: 0.73. SMARKETS DATA PENDING — must pull ARG-AUT full_time_result market and average with crowd consensus before final submission.
Q5_argentina_win.gd1_context: ARG-ALG crowd priced ARG at 75%, actual outcome YES — crowd well-calibrated here
Q5_argentina_win.smarkets_status: PENDING — ask user permission before API call (10k daily quota)
Q5_argentina_win.edge_direction: ARG is underpriced by crowd formula; preliminary estimate above crowd
Q5_argentina_win.preliminary_note: Use 0.73 if Smarkets unavailable. Update to avg(crowd, Smarkets) once data arrives.

Q6_total_2_or_fewer_goals.question: Will the match have 2 or fewer total goals?
Q6_total_2_or_fewer_goals.method: Poisson double-Poisson: P(0)=0.077, P(1)=0.197, P(2)=0.253, total P(<=2) = 52.7%. AUT defensive depth will suppress scoring compared to ARG-ALG (3-0) and AUT-JOR (3-1) — those opponents were weaker. This is a different regime: AUT will park and defend, ARG will grind. Crowd likely prices 42-48% (under-prices low-scoring probability given both teams just scored 3 in GD1).
Q6_total_2_or_fewer_goals.note_gd1_context: AUT-JOR had 4 goals and ARG-ALG had 3 goals, but those vs weaker opponents. AUT will be MUCH more defensive vs ARG.
Q6_total_2_or_fewer_goals.edge_direction: YES edge: model 52.7%, crowd likely 45% → ~8pp gap

Q7_4plus_total_cards.question: Will there be 4 or more total cards shown?
Q7_4plus_total_cards.method: Poisson: lambda_cards = 2.5 (WC average 2.86, reduced for dominant match pattern). P(>=4) = 24.2%. AUT-JOR CONFIRMS: 4+ cards = NO (crowd 44%) in a 3-1 Austria win. Pattern: dominant team wins cleanly = fewer cards. GER-CUR (0 cards) and AUT-JOR (<4 cards) both confirm. Crowd likely 0.35-0.42 — we stay below.
Q7_4plus_total_cards.aut_jor_confirmation: Austria won 3-1 vs Jordan with fewer than 4 total cards (crowd 44%, outcome NO)
Q7_4plus_total_cards.ger_cur_confirmation: Germany beat Curacao with 0 total cards in dominant win
Q7_4plus_total_cards.edge_direction: NO edge: model 24% vs crowd ~38% = 14pp gap

Q8_argentina_6plus_sot.question: Will Argentina have 6 or more shots on target?
Q8_argentina_6plus_sot.method: Poisson: lambda_ARG_SOT = 1.9254 * 3.3 = 6.35 (revised multiplier from 3.7 to 3.3 after AUT-JOR warning). P(>=6) = 60.9%. AUT-JOR WARNING: Austria (dominant, 3-1 win, 72% favorites) had FEWER than 6 SOT (crowd 50%, outcome NO). However ARG is more dominant than AUT was vs JOR, and vs a harder opponent. Crowd typically inflates winner SOT props by +10-17pp above formula. Preliminary estimate at lower end of revised range.
Q8_argentina_6plus_sot.aut_jor_warning: Austria won 3-1 but had <6 SOT even as dominant team (crowd 50%, outcome NO) — calibrates SOT multiplier downward
Q8_argentina_6plus_sot.edge_direction: NO lean: model 61-71%, crowd likely 73% — moderate edge on NO side

Q9_argentina_scores_2h.question: Will Argentina score in the second half?
Q9_argentina_scores_2h.method: Poisson: lambda_ARG_2H = 1.9254 * 0.55 = 1.059. P(ARG scores in 2H) = 1-e^-1.059 = 65.3%. ARG-ALG: Argentina scored 3 total goals, almost certainly scored in 2H. AUT-JOR Q5: Austria scored in 2H = YES (crowd 65%) — general pattern that dominant favorites score in 2H is confirmed.
Q9_argentina_scores_2h.arg_alg_context: ARG scored 3 vs Algeria — highly likely scored in 2H
Q9_argentina_scores_2h.aut_jor_pattern: Dominant team (AUT) scored in 2H vs Jordan = YES (crowd 65%) — same question type
Q9_argentina_scores_2h.edge_direction: YES lean — model and crowd aligned here, small YES edge from high-line ARG style

Q10_sabitzer_1plus_sot.question: Will Marcel Sabitzer have at least 1 shot on target?
Q10_sabitzer_1plus_sot.method: RULE15 + RULE7 + GD1 personal data. RULE15: AUT <15% underdog -> player SOT suppressed, estimate 0.25-0.35 regardless of model. RULE7: extreme underdog, further suppression. GD1 BOMBSHELL: Sabitzer had 0 SOT when Austria was the DOMINANT team (won 3-1 vs Jordan, crowd 57%). If he doesn't shoot even as dominant team, in this match as massive underdog he will have even fewer opportunities. λ_Sabitzer = Austria_total_SOT * Sabitzer_share = 2.37 * 0.25 = 0.59 base, BUT revised DOWN to ~0.30 given GD1 0-SOT data. P(>=1 SOT) at lambda=0.30: 25.9%.

gd1_sabitzer_data.interpretation: Sabitzer does not shoot regardless of match situation. In underdog role, will have even fewer opportunities.

Q10_sabitzer_1plus_sot.crowd_expected_range: 0.33-0.42 (anchored on Austria's roster role, unaware of GD1 0-SOT data)
Q10_sabitzer_1plus_sot.edge_direction: STRONGEST EDGE: model+GD1 = 0.16-0.22, crowd likely 0.35-0.42 = 15-20pp gap on NO side

post_match_results.match_score: ARG 1-0 AUT (LIVE at time of Q9 settlement)

match_facts.note: Match live, 1-0 Argentina at time of first question settlement (Q9). Sabitzer recorded 1+ SOT as underdog — DIRECTLY CONTRADICTS our GD1 evidence from AUT-JOR where he had 0 SOT as dominant player. KEY LEARNING: Sabitzer's 0-SOT pattern in AUT-JOR was when Austria WAS THE DOMINANT TEAM — the trailing/desperate context in ARG-AUT may have actually INCREASED his shot attempts.

Q6_4plus_total_cards.notes: 4+ cards = YES in a 2-0 Argentina win. AUT-JOR pattern (dominant win = <4 cards) DID NOT transfer. Root cause: AUT-JOR was 3-1, Austria dominant. ARG-AUT was 2-0, Austria fighting hard to come back from 1-0 deficit. Competitive underdog context → more physical play → more cards. SAME ROOT CAUSE AS Q9 SABITZER: we modeled this as a blowout when it was a competitive match. Both Q6 AND Q9 failed for the same reason.

Q1_aut_2plus_offsides.notes: Austria <2 offsides: NO. Team-level RULE7 worked — AUT didn't accumulate offsides in chasing mode.

Q2_aut_more_2h_sot.notes: Austria NOT more 2H SOT than ARG: NO. Team-level RULE7 confirmed. Even in competitive match, ARG dominated SOT.

Q3_2h_more_goals_than_1h.notes: 2H NOT more goals than 1H: NO. 1 goal each half (ARG scored 1H, 1 in 2H). Model-based, no rule override.

Q4_aut_more_corners.notes: AUSTRIA HAD MORE CORNERS THAN ARGENTINA IN A 2-0 LOSS. Corner reversal: Austria chased from 1-0 deficit throughout match, repeatedly attacked, got repelled by ARG, accumulated corners. ARG at 2-0 sat back and defended — didn't need corners. LESSON: in competitive match where trailing team chases late, CHASING team accumulates corners from failed attacks. 'Dominant team gets more corners' only holds in genuine blowouts where dominant team keeps attacking. In a managed 2-0, the chasing team is the one attacking into corners.

Q5_total_le2_goals.notes: ≤2 total goals: YES. Match ended 2-0. Model was right that AUT defensive setup would suppress total goals.

Q7_arg_6plus_sot.notes: ARG scored 2 goals but had FEWER THAN 6 SOT. This is now a pattern: AUT scored 3 in AUT-JOR with <6 SOT, ARG scored 2 here with <6 SOT. Elite teams in managed wins are CLINICAL — high goal-to-SOT conversion, low total SOT. SOT multiplier of 3.3-3.7x lambda may be too high. Revise down to 2.8-3.0x for elite teams expected to win by 2-goal margin.

Q8_arg_scores_2h.notes: ARG scored in 2H. Small win due to identical crowd estimate.

Q9_sabitzer_1plus_sot.notes: RULE15 FAILED. Sabitzer got 1+ SOT as a massive underdog (AUT 8.7% win prob). Our GD1 evidence (0 SOT in AUT-JOR) was from Sabitzer as DOMINANT team — NOT as underdog. Competitive chasing context = more shots. Crowd 43% was significantly closer to truth than our 18%.

post_match_results.q10_note: Argentina win (Q10): we did NOT submit an estimate (Smarkets data was pending). Crowd 70% was correct. Platform showed 'The Crowd called it perfectly!' We missed a small positive RBP here.
post_match_results.UNIFIED_LESSON_ARG_AUT: Q6 (cards) and Q9 (Sabitzer SOT) failed for IDENTICAL reasons: we assumed a blowout match script (like AUT-JOR 3-1) but ARG-AUT was a COMPETITIVE 2-0. Austria fought the whole match. Competitive underdogs trailing 1-0 or 2-0 = more cards (fighting to come back) AND more player SOT (desperate captain). Our AUT-JOR calibration assumed Austria would be comfortable — wrong context. Going forward: if expected winning margin is 2 goals or fewer, treat as COMPETITIVE match. Only suppress cards/player-SOT in genuine BLOWOUT territory (3+ goal expected margin).

Q1.notes: ARG high-line confirmed; AUT runners in chasing mode

Q2.notes: RULE7 + AUT-JOR confirmation: extreme underdogs get fewer 2H SOT

Q4.notes: RULE7 + AUT-JOR: dominant team always gets more corners

Q5.notes: PRELIMINARY - pending Smarkets for RULE1 avg. Use 0.73 if no API pull.

Q6.notes: Model 52.7%; crowd likely under-prices due to GD1 high-scoring bias

Q7.notes: AUT-JOR confirms: dominant wins have <4 cards

Q8.notes: AUT-JOR warns: dominant teams can fall below 6 SOT; revised multiplier

Q9.notes: ARG form + lambda; dominant favorites score in 2H pattern

Q10.notes: BOMBSHELL: Sabitzer 0 SOT as dominant player. Best edge in batch.
